citations.description: Ticket status is in_progress; only comment states 'NOT yet implemented' at 2026-07-12.

findings.criterion: make lint passes on the change.
findings.detail: This criterion requires executing `make lint` (ruff check + ruff format --check) against the implementation. The repository contains no recorded attestation that this command was run and passed on the implemented change. The ticket is still in_progress and the only comment explicitly states the work was 'NOT yet implemented' at comment time. I can read the source files but cannot execute build commands, so I cannot confirm this criterion is met.
findings.remediation: Run `make lint` against the branch and record the exit-0 result (or tag this criterion [operator-attested] and attach a concrete CI run URL/id with the pass result).

findings.criterion: make test passes on the change.

The new test files exist (test_a8e5_absence_veto_edge.py, test_a8e5_det_hygiene_edge.py, test_a8e5_operator_attested_edge.py) but whether they pass when executed is unverifiable from source reading alone.
findings.remediation: Run `make test` against the branch and record the exit-0 result (or tag this criterion [operator-attested] and attach a concrete CI run URL/id with the pass result).

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
